---
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
bibliography: refs.bib

---

```{r, Packages}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(lubridate)
library(FSA)
library(gt)
library(tinytable)
library(here)
library(english)
```

```{r, define spell_nr}

spell_nr <- function(x){
  if (x <= 9){
    as.character(english::english(x))
  } else{
    x
  } 
}
```

```{r, create full df}
#| include: false
# Get the boat data from the data cleaning script for debugging only
# boat <- readRDS("./temp/boat_data.rds")
# 
# boat <- boat %>% 
#   select(date.x, species, group_size, behaviour, geometry) %>% 
#   mutate(
#     source = "boat"
#   )
# 
# land <- data %>% 
#   select(date.x, species, group_size, behaviour, sighting_poi) %>% 
#   rename(geometry = sighting_poi) %>% 
#   mutate(
#     source = "land"
#   )
# 
# full <- boat %>%
#   bind_rows(land)
# 
# saveRDS(full, "./temp/full.rds")
```



```{r, Read data}
#| include: false
# Excel sheet of the spots with details
spots <- read_excel(here("data", "datasheet_landsurvey.xlsx"),sheet ="spots")


# Clean the sighting data and import
source("./land_survey.R")

full <- readRDS("./temp/full.rds")


# Set active spots as variable
active_spots <- spots %>%  
  filter(spot_name != "MedSteps" & spot_name != "WestSide") %>% 
  select(spot_name)

odontoceti <- c("common_dolphin", "striped_dolphin", "bottlenose_dolphin", "delphinidae")
mysteciti <- c("fin_whale")

```

```{r}
#|include: false

by_species <- data %>% 
  summarise(
    n_species = n(),
    .by = "species"
  ) %>% 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = species,
    values_from = n_species
  )

by_familiy <- full %>% 
  filter(source == "land") %>% 
  mutate(family = case_when(
    species %in% odontoceti ~ "odontoceti",
    species %in% mysteciti ~ "mysteciti",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>% 
  summarise(
    sighting = n(),
    .by = "family"
  ) %>% 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = family,
    values_from = sighting
  )


per_spot <- data %>% 
  summarise(
    sightings = n(),
    .by = "spot_name"
  )


full_source <- full %>% 
  summarise(
    sightings = n(),
    .by = "source"
  ) %>% 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = source,
    values_from = sightings
  )

full_sum <- full %>% 
  summarise(
    sightings = n()
  )

boat_species <- full %>% 
  filter(source == "boat") %>% 
  summarise(
    n_species = n(),
    .by = "species"
  ) %>% 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = species,
    values_from = n_species
  )

boat <- full %>% 
  filter(source == "boat")


```

```{r, Effort analysis}
#|include: false

effort <- effort_log %>% 
  mutate(date = dmy(date)) %>% 
  filter(date > "2026-05-28") %>% 
  mutate(across(c(observation_start, observation_end),
                ~ hms::as_hms(.))) %>% 
  filter(!grepl("off_effort", survey_id)) %>% 
  mutate(effort_time = as.duration(observation_end - observation_start))

er <- data %>%  
  filter(spot_name %in% active_spots$spot_name) %>% 
  summarise(min_group = min(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            max_group = max(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            mean_group = mean(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            median_group = median(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_group = sd(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            min_dis = min(distance, na.rm = TRUE),
            max_dis = max(distance, na.rm = TRUE),
            sightings = n(),
            .by = "spot_name") %>%
  left_join(effort %>%
              group_by(spot_name) %>% 
  summarise(hours = sum(effort_time)/60^2), by = "spot_name") %>% 
  mutate(per_hour = round(sightings/hours, 2)) %>% 
  mutate(group_range = paste(min_group, "-", max_group)) %>% 
  select(spot_name, sightings, hours, per_hour, min_dis, max_dis, group_range, mean_group, median_group, sd_group) %>% 
  arrange(desc(per_hour))


total_land <- effort %>% 
  summarise(
    surveys = n()
  )

species <- data %>%  
  filter(spot_name %in% active_spots$spot_name) %>% 
  summarise(max_group = max(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            min_group = min(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
            max_dis = max(distance, na.rm = TRUE),
            min_dis = min(distance, na.rm = TRUE),
            sightings = n(),
            .by = "species")  %>% 
  arrange(desc(sightings))


```

```{r, Overview Table by Spot}
library(dplyr)
library(tinytable)

## --- helpers -----------------------------------------------------------------
## NA (not Inf) for all-missing subsets; en-dash range, em-dash for empty.
smin <- function(x) { x <- x[!is.na(x)]; if (length(x)) min(x) else NA_real_ }
smax <- function(x) { x <- x[!is.na(x)]; if (length(x)) max(x) else NA_real_ }
rng  <- function(a, b) ifelse(is.na(a) | is.na(b), "—", paste0(a, " – ", b))
## NA/NaN-safe numeric formatting (single-sighting species -> SD is NA)
fmt  <- function(x, d) ifelse(is.na(x), "—", sprintf(paste0("%.", d, "f"), x))

pretty_species <- function(x) {
  map <- c(common_dolphin = "Common Dolphin",
           delphinidae     = "Delphinidae",
           fin_whale       = "Fin Whale",
           unidentified    = "Unidentified")
  out  <- unname(map[x])
  miss <- is.na(out)
  out[miss] <- tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", x[miss]))
  out
}

active <- data %>% filter(spot_name %in% active_spots$spot_name)

## --- 1. spot-level summary (== your `er` table) ------------------------------
spot_sum <- active %>%
  summarise(
    sightings    = n(),
    d_min        = round(smin(distance)),
    d_max        = round(smax(distance)),
    g_min        = smin(group_size),
    g_max        = smax(group_size),
    g_mean       = mean(group_size,   na.rm = TRUE),
    g_median     = median(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
    g_sd         = sd(group_size,     na.rm = TRUE),
    .by = spot_name
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    effort %>% group_by(spot_name) %>%
      summarise(hours = sum(effort_time) / 60^2, .groups = "drop"),
    by = "spot_name"
  ) %>%
  mutate(per_hour = round(sightings / hours, 2),
         hours    = round(hours, 1)) %>%
  arrange(desc(per_hour))            # spot ordering = descending encounter rate

## --- 2. species-level split within each spot ---------------------------------
sp_sum <- active %>%
  summarise(
    sightings = n(),
    d_min     = round(smin(distance)),
    d_max     = round(smax(distance)),
    g_min     = smin(group_size),
    g_max     = smax(group_size),
    g_mean    = mean(group_size,   na.rm = TRUE),
    g_median  = median(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),
    g_sd      = sd(group_size,     na.rm = TRUE),
    .by = c(spot_name, species)
  ) %>%
  arrange(match(spot_name, spot_sum$spot_name), desc(sightings))

## --- 3. build interleaved display rows ---------------------------------------
spot_order <- tibble(spot_name = spot_sum$spot_name,
                     .ord = seq_along(spot_sum$spot_name))

spot_rows <- spot_sum %>%
  transmute(
    spot_name,
    label                = spot_name,
    Sightings            = as.character(sightings),
    `Distance range (m)` = rng(d_min, d_max),
    `Group range`        = rng(g_min, g_max),
    Hours                = sprintf("%.1f", hours),
    `Sightings·h⁻¹`      = sprintf("%.2f", per_hour),
    `Mean group`         = fmt(g_mean, 2),
    `Median group`       = fmt(g_median, 1),
    `SD group`           = fmt(g_sd, 2),
    .is_spot = TRUE, .sub = 0
  )

sp_rows <- sp_sum %>%
  transmute(
    spot_name,
    label                = paste0("\u2003", pretty_species(species)),   # indent species
    Sightings            = as.character(sightings),
    `Distance range (m)` = rng(d_min, d_max),
    `Group range`        = rng(g_min, g_max),
    Hours = "", `Sightings·h⁻¹` = "",
    `Mean group`         = fmt(g_mean, 2),
    `Median group`       = fmt(g_median, 1),
    `SD group`           = fmt(g_sd, 2),
    .is_spot = FALSE
  ) %>%
  group_by(spot_name) %>% mutate(.sub = row_number()) %>% ungroup()

combined <- bind_rows(spot_rows, sp_rows) %>%
  left_join(spot_order, by = "spot_name") %>%
  arrange(.ord, .sub)

spot_pos <- which(combined$.is_spot)          # row indices to emphasise

disp <- combined %>%
  select("Spot / Species" = label, Sightings, Hours,
         "Sightings per hour" = `Sightings·h⁻¹`,
         "Distance from observer" = `Distance range (m)`,
         "Range"  = `Group range`,
         "Mean"   = `Mean group`,
         "Median" = `Median group`,
         "SD"     = `SD group`)

## --- 4. render ---------------------------------------------------------------
## columns: 1 label | 2 Sightings | 3 Hours | 4 rate | 5 Distance | 6-9 Group block
overview_tbl <- tt(
  disp, width = c(4.7, 2, 1.3, 3, 4, 2.5, 2, 2, 2)
) %>%
  group_tt(j = list("Group Size" = 6:9)) %>%      # spanner over Range/Mean/Median/SD
  style_tt(j = 2:9, align = "c") %>%
  style_tt(i = spot_pos, bold = TRUE, background = "#212842", color = "white") %>%
  style_tt(i = -1:0, bold = TRUE)                 # bold spanner + header rows
#overview_tbl

## Optional export
# save_tt(overview_tbl, "summary_table.html", overwrite = TRUE)  # or .docx / .tex / .png
```


```{r}
#| include: false
#| output: false

effort <- effort_log %>%
  filter(!str_detect(survey_id, "off_effort")) %>%
  mutate(date = dmy(date)) %>% 
  filter(date > "2026-05-28") %>% 
  mutate(
    duration_min = as.numeric(difftime(observation_end, observation_start,
                                        units = "mins"))
  )
 
# ---- 3. Detections: keep on-effort sightings -------------------------------
sightings <- detection %>%
  filter(on_effort == 1)

 
# ---- 4. Sightings & individuals per scan -----------------------------------
per_scan <- sightings %>%
  group_by(survey_id) %>%
  summarise(
    n_sightings   = n(),
    n_individuals = sum(group_size, na.rm = TRUE),  # one NA group size -> 0
    .groups = "drop"
  )
 
# Join back onto the full effort table; scans with no sighting become 0 (NOT NA)
effort <- effort %>%
  left_join(per_scan, by = "survey_id") %>%
  mutate(
    n_sightings   = replace_na(n_sightings, 0),
    n_individuals = replace_na(n_individuals, 0)
  )
 
# ---- 5. SPUE by spot -------------------------------------------------------
spue_by_spot <- effort %>%
  group_by(spot_name) %>%
  summarise(
    scans        = n(),
    effort_hours = sum(duration_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    sightings    = sum(n_sightings),
    individuals  = sum(n_individuals),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    spue_per_scan   = sightings / scans,        # headline SPUE
    sightings_per_h = sightings / effort_hours, # encounter rate
    indiv_per_h     = individuals / effort_hours
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(scans))
 
# ---- 6. SPUE overall -------------------------------------------------------
spue_overall <- effort %>%
  summarise(
    spot_name    = "TOTAL",
    scans        = n(),
    effort_hours = sum(duration_min, na.rm = TRUE) / 60,
    sightings    = sum(n_sightings),
    individuals  = sum(n_individuals)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    spue_per_scan   = sightings / scans,
    sightings_per_h = sightings / effort_hours,
    indiv_per_h     = individuals / effort_hours
  )
 
spue_summary <- bind_rows(spue_by_spot, spue_overall)
 
# ---- 7. Report & export ----------------------------------------------------
spue_report <- spue_summary %>%
  mutate(across(c(effort_hours, spue_per_scan, sightings_per_h, indiv_per_h),
                ~ round(.x, 3)))
 
#print(spue_report, n = Inf)



```

```{r, Kruskal-Wallis and Dunn test}
 #|include: false

# Include the right df from datavis script

 active <- c("Europa", "SandyBay", "PrinceGeorge")
 scans  <- effort %>% filter(spot_name %in% active) # per-scan rows, NOT the summary

# count(scans, spot_name)


 SPUE_kru <- kruskal.test(n_sightings ~ spot_name, data = scans)

 SPUE_dunn <- dunnTest(n_sightings ~ factor(spot_name), data = scans, method = "bh")

```

```{r, Distance function and test}
 #|include: false
 #|echo: false
 #|output: false
 #|fig-show: hide

library(Distance)

dis <- readRDS(here("temp", "dis.rds"))
trunc <- 8000
conversion.factor <- convert_units("Metre", NULL, "square metre")

# Overall detection function (pooled)
d.hn <- ds(data=dis %>% filter(!is.na(dis$size)), key="hn", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=trunc)
d.hr <- ds(data=dis %>% filter(!is.na(dis$size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=trunc)


# Significant is good so it does not fit the data
gof_hn <- gof_ds(d.hn, plot = FALSE)

# P value is not significant, good
gof_hr <- gof_ds(d.hr, plot = FALSE)

# height as a covariate
d.ht <- ds(data=dis %>% filter(!is.na(dis$size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=trunc, formula = ~height)
# Region as covariate
d.ss <- ds(data=dis %>% filter(!is.na(dis$size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=trunc, formula = ~sea_state)
#Region and height
d.wh <- ds(data=dis %>% filter(!is.na(dis$size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=trunc, formula =~wave_height)
d.ws <- ds(data=dis %>% filter(!is.na(dis$size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=trunc, formula = ~wind_speed)
d.gl <- ds(data=dis %>% filter(!is.na(dis$size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=trunc, formula = ~glare)


# Write summary table
model_tab <- summarize_ds_models(d.hn, d.hr, d.ht, d.ss, d.wh, d.ws, d.gl, output = "plain")

# Summary of the best model and abundance and density estimates
sum_ht <- summary(d.ht)


```

```{r, Distance per spot}
 #|include: false

# Europa Point ------
europa <- dis %>% filter(Region.Label == "Europa")

eu.hn <- ds(data=europa %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hn", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=4500)
eu.hr <- ds(data=europa %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=4500)

eu.AIC <- AIC(eu.hn, eu.hr)

# Significant is good so it does not fit the data
gof_hn_eu <- gof_ds(eu.hn, plot = FALSE)
# P value is not significant, good
gof_hr_eu <- gof_ds(eu.hr, plot = FALSE)

# covariates
eu.gl <- ds(data=europa %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=4500, formula = ~glare)
eu.ss <- ds(data=europa %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=4500, formula = ~sea_state)
eu.wh <- ds(data=europa %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=4500, formula = ~wave_height)
eu.ws <- ds(data=europa %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=4500, formula = ~wind_speed)
# Write summary table
model_europa <- summarize_ds_models(eu.hn, eu.hr, eu.gl, eu.ss, eu.wh, eu.ws, output = "plain")

# Best model
bf.europa <- summary(eu.ss)

# Sandy Bay ------
sandy <- dis %>% filter(Region.Label == "SandyBay")

sb.hn <- ds(data=sandy %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hn", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=12000)
sb.hr <- ds(data=sandy %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=12000)

sb.AIC <- AIC(sb.hn, sb.hr)

# Significant is good so it does not fit the data
gof_hn_sb <- gof_ds(sb.hn, plot = FALSE)
# P value is not significant, good
gof_hr_sb <- gof_ds(sb.hr, plot = FALSE)

# covariates
sb.gl <- ds(data=sandy %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=12000, formula = ~glare)
sb.ss <- ds(data=sandy %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=12000, formula = ~sea_state)
sb.wh <- ds(data=sandy %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=12000, formula = ~wave_height)
sb.ws <- ds(data=sandy %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=12000, formula = ~wind_speed)

model_sandy <- summarize_ds_models(sb.hn, sb.hr, sb.gl, sb.ss, sb.wh, sb.ws, output = "plain")

# Best model
bf.sandy <- summary(sb.hr)

# Prince George ------
prince <- dis %>% filter(Region.Label == "PrinceGeorge")

pg.hn <- ds(data=prince %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hn", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=8000)
pg.hr <- ds(data=prince %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hr", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=8000)

pg.AIC <- AIC(pg.hn, pg.hr)

# Significant is good so it does not fit the data
gof_hn_pg <- gof_ds(pg.hn, plot = FALSE)
# P value is not significant, good
gof_hr_pg <- gof_ds(pg.hr, plot = FALSE)

# covariates
pg.gl <- ds(data=prince %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hn", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=8000, formula = ~glare)
pg.ss <- ds(data=prince %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hn", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=8000, formula = ~sea_state)
pg.wh <- ds(data=prince %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hn", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=8000, formula = ~wave_height)
pg.ws <- ds(data=prince %>% filter(!is.na(size)), key="hn", adjustment= NULL,
              transect="point", convert_units=conversion.factor, truncation=8000, formula = ~wind_speed)

model_prince <- summarize_ds_models(pg.hn, pg.hr, pg.gl, pg.ss, pg.wh, pg.ws, output = "plain")

# Best model
bf.prince <- summary(pg.hn)

```


```{r, Clean ds data}
 #|include: false


# # Pooled data

pool_group_dens <- sum_ht$dht$clusters$D %>%
  transmute(Label,
            "Density (km⁻²)"   = Estimate * 1e6,
            "SE (km⁻²)" = se * 1e6,
            "LCL (km⁻²)" = lcl * 1e6,
            "UCL (km⁻²)" = ucl * 1e6,
            CV = cv)

pool_indiv_dens <- d.ht$dht$individuals$D %>%
  transmute(Label,
            "Density (km⁻²)"   = Estimate * 1e6,
            "SE (km⁻²)" = se * 1e6,
            "LCL (km⁻²)" = lcl * 1e6,
            "UCL (km⁻²)" = ucl * 1e6,
            CV = cv)
# 
# # Abundance

pool <- sum_ht          # <- single pooled fit; change if d.ht is the intended one

# n of groups per stratum (+ Total) from the clusters summary
n_tbl <- pool$dht$clusters$summary %>% transmute(Label = Region, n_groups = n)

# helper: pull a D or N block, tag columns, keep Label as the key
pull_block <- function(df, scale = 1) {
  df %>% transmute(Label,
                   Estimate = Estimate * scale,
                   SE = se * scale,
                   LCL = lcl * scale,
                   UCL = ucl * scale,
                   CV  = cv)
}

grp_dens <- pull_block(pool$dht$clusters$D,    1e6)   # km^-2
ind_dens <- pull_block(pool$dht$individuals$D, 1e6)   # km^-2
grp_abun <- pull_block(pool$dht$clusters$N,    1)     # counts
ind_abun <- pull_block(pool$dht$individuals$N, 1)     # counts

pool_overview_tbl <- n_tbl %>%
  left_join(grp_dens, by = "Label") %>%
  left_join(ind_dens, by = "Label", suffix = c(".gd", ".id")) %>%
  left_join(grp_abun, by = "Label") %>%
  left_join(ind_abun, by = "Label", suffix = c(".ga", ".ia")) %>%
  rename(Station = Label)

# rename to the exact column set your per-spot table used
names(pool_overview_tbl) <- c(
  "Station", "n of Groups",
  "Group Dens. Estimate","Group Dens. SE","Group Dens. LCL","Group Dens. UCL","Group Dens. CV",
  "Indiv. Dens. Estimate","Indiv. Dens. SE","Indiv. Dens. LCL","Indiv. Dens. UCL","Indiv. Dens. CV",
  "Group Abun. Estimate","Group Abun. SE","Group Abun. LCL","Group Abun. UCL","Group Abun. CV",
  "Indiv. Abun. Estimate","Indiv. Abun. SE","Indiv. Abun. LCL","Indiv. Abun. UCL","Indiv. Abun. CV"
)

pooled_tbl <- as.data.frame(pool_overview_tbl, check.names = FALSE)
colnames(pooled_tbl) <- c("Station","n of Groups", rep(c("Estimate","SE","LCL","UCL","CV"), 4))

pool_dens_tbl <- pooled_tbl[, c(1, 2, 3:12)]
names(pool_dens_tbl) <- c("Station", "n of Groups",
                          rep(c("Estimate","SE","LCL","UCL","CV"), 2))

pool_abun_tbl <- pooled_tbl[, c(1, 2, 13:22)]
names(pool_abun_tbl) <- c("Station", "n of Groups",
                          rep(c("Estimate","SE","LCL","UCL","CV"), 2))

```

```{r, Spot overview table}

d.models <- list(
  PrinceGeorge = bf.prince,
  Europa       = bf.europa,
  SandyBay     = bf.sandy
)

extract_model <- function(model, station){
  cl <- model$dht$clusters
  ind <- model$dht$individuals
  tibble(
    Station        = station,
    n_groups       = cl$summary$n,
    # --- group (cluster) density ---
    group_dens_es  = cl$D$Estimate,
    group_dens_se  = cl$D$se,
    group_dens_lcl = cl$D$lcl,
    group_dens_ucl = cl$D$ucl,
    group_dens_cv  = cl$D$cv,
    # --- individual density ---
    indiv_dens_es  = ind$D$Estimate,
    indiv_dens_se  = ind$D$se,
    indiv_dens_lcl = ind$D$lcl,
    indiv_dens_ucl = ind$D$ucl,
    indiv_dens_cv  = ind$D$cv,
    # --- group (cluster) abundance ---
    group_abun_es  = cl$N$Estimate,
    group_abun_se  = cl$N$se,
    group_abun_lcl = cl$N$lcl,
    group_abun_ucl = cl$N$ucl,
    group_abun_cv  = cl$N$cv,
    # --- individual abundance ---
    indiv_abun_es  = ind$N$Estimate,
    indiv_abun_se  = ind$N$se,
    indiv_abun_lcl = ind$N$lcl,
    indiv_abun_ucl = ind$N$ucl,
    indiv_abun_cv  = ind$N$cv
  )
}

model_overview_tbl <- imap_dfr(d.models, extract_model)

clean_overview_tbl <- model_overview_tbl %>% 
  transmute(
  Station = Station,
  "n of Groups" = n_groups,

  # --- Group density (km^-2) ---
  "Group Dens. Estimate" = group_dens_es  * 1e6,
  "Group Dens. SE"       = group_dens_se  * 1e6,
  "Group Dens. LCL"      = group_dens_lcl * 1e6,
  "Group Dens. UCL"      = group_dens_ucl * 1e6,
  "Group Dens. CV"       = group_dens_cv,          

  # --- Individual density (km^-2) ---
  "Indiv. Dens. Estimate" = indiv_dens_es  * 1e6,
  "Indiv. Dens. SE"       = indiv_dens_se  * 1e6,
  "Indiv. Dens. LCL"      = indiv_dens_lcl * 1e6,
  "Indiv. Dens. UCL"      = indiv_dens_ucl * 1e6,
  "Indiv. Dens. CV"       = indiv_dens_cv,          

  # --- Group abundance (counts: no unit conversion) ---
  "Group Abun. Estimate" = group_abun_es,
  "Group Abun. SE"       = group_abun_se,
  "Group Abun. LCL"      = group_abun_lcl,
  "Group Abun. UCL"      = group_abun_ucl,
  "Group Abun. CV"       = group_abun_cv,

  # --- Individual abundance (counts: no unit conversion) ---
  "Indiv. Abun. Estimate" = indiv_abun_es,
  "Indiv. Abun. SE"       = indiv_abun_se,
  "Indiv. Abun. LCL"      = indiv_abun_lcl,
  "Indiv. Abun. UCL"      = indiv_abun_ucl,
  "Indiv. Abun. CV"       = indiv_abun_cv
)

dens_tbl <- clean_overview_tbl[, c(1, 2, 3:12)]
names(dens_tbl) <- c("Station", "n of Groups",
                          rep(c("Estimate","SE","LCL","UCL","CV"), 2))


abun_tbl <- clean_overview_tbl[, c(1, 2, 13:22)]
names(abun_tbl) <- c("Station", "n of Groups",
                          rep(c("Estimate","SE","LCL","UCL","CV"), 2))



```


This study applied point transect distance sampling technique to small cetaceans, utilising a modified version of the point transect protocol primarily employed for birds or larger cetaceans [@bucklandIntroductionDistanceSampling2001a]. The protocol was adapted to incorporate land-based surveys, enabling a systematic assessment of the cetacean distribution in the `#gls("bgtw")`{=typst}.

## Survey Results
Overall, `r  full_sum$sightings` cetacean sightings were recorded between `r format(min(full$date.x), "%d %b %Y")` and `r format(max(full$date.x), "%d %b %Y")`, point transect sampling was conducted including pilot studies and both vessel- and land-based surveys (@fig-totalmap). A total of two vessel-based surveys were conducted, accounting for `r spell_nr(full_source$boat)` of these sightings: `r spell_nr(boat_species$common_dolphin)` sightings of common dolphins (*Delphinus delphis*) and `r spell_nr(boat_species$striped_dolphin)` sightings of striped dolphins (*Stenella coeruleoalba*) and `r spell_nr(boat_species$delphinidae)` sighting where the species could not be identified. The group size for vessel-based sightings ranged from `r spell_nr(min(boat$group_size, na.rm = TRUE))` to `r spell_nr(max(boat$group_size, na.rm = TRUE))` (mean = `r round(mean(boat$group_size, na.rm = TRUE), 2)`, median = `r round(median(boat$group_size, na.rm = TRUE), 2)`, SD = ±`r round(sd(boat$group_size, na.rm = TRUE), 2)`).

![Geographic location of cetacean sightings recorded from boat-based surveys (in squares) and land-based surveys (in circles) during this project period. Where n represents the number of sightings recorded using each method.](figures/method_map.png){#fig-totalmap}

In total `r total_land$surveys` land-based surveys were conducted, resulting in `r round(sum(er$hours), 2)` total on-effort hours and 58 off-effort hours over `r length(unique(effort$date))` survey days. `r length(data$survey_id)` cetacean sightings were recorded in this sampling period, of which `r sum(data$on_effort)` were on-effort and `r spell_nr(sum(!data$on_effort))` off-effort. Of these, `r by_familiy$odontoceti` sightings were Odontoceti, including `r by_species$common_dolphin` sightings of common dolphins, `r by_species$delphinidae` sightings of unidentified dolphins where the species could not certainly be defined and `r spell_nr(by_familiy$mysteciti)` Mysticeti sightings of  Fin whales (*Balaenoptera physalus*). `r spell_nr(by_species$unidentified)` sightings the species could not be reliably identified and were recorded as unidentified (@fig-sight_map).

![All sightings collected from land-based surveys between `r format(min(full$date.x), "%d.%m.%Y")` and `r format(max(full$date.x), "%d.%m.%Y")`. The sightings were recorded from three different spots, Europa Point, Sandy Bay and Prince George's Battery (indicated as triangles).](figures/sightings_legend.png){#fig-sight_map}

The group size for land-based sightings ranged from `r min(data$group_size, na.rm = TRUE)` to `r max(data$group_size, na.rm = TRUE)` (mean = `r round(mean(data$group_size, na.rm = TRUE), 2)`, median = `r round(median(data$group_size, na.rm = TRUE), 2)`, SD = ±`r round(sd(data$group_size, na.rm = TRUE), 2)`). The overview table (@tbl-overview) showed different maximum and minimum distances as well as varying group sizes across the spots, with the farthest sighting being `r round(max(er$max_dis)/1000,2)` km away from the observation platform. The number of sightings differed significantly among sampling locations, despite equal on-effort observation time across all locations.


The most common species recorded was the Common Dolphin (n = `r species$sightings[1]`), followed by unidentified dolphins (n = `r species$sightings[2]`). The farthest sightings were unidentified dolphins at around `r round(max(species$max_dis/1000),2)` km (@tbl-overview).

```{r}
#| label: tbl-overview
#| tbl-cap: "Statistics per observation platforms over the sampling period. Pilot studies were excluded in this table. Distance is calculated from the respective observation platform."

overview_tbl
```


The number of sightings was also plotted against the distance they were recorded in a histogram (@fig-dis_spot). With a clear trend of sightings at Europa being closer to the observer compared to the other spots.

![Number of sightings against the Distance (in m) where they were recorded relative to the observation spot. Split up by the observation spots (SandyBay, Europa and PrinceGeorge (binwidth = 550)).](figures/distance_facet.png){#fig-dis_spot}

Most of the sightings were recorded at `r per_spot$spot_name[which.max(per_spot$sightings)]`, covering mainly the southern side (n = `r max(per_spot$sightings)`). And between the hours XX and XX (@fig-day_SPUE A). `#gls("spue")`{=typst} were highest at Europa (@fig-day_SPUE B, @tbl-spue_report). A Kruskal-Wallis test showed a significant difference in `#gls("spue")`{=typst} among spots (χ² = `r round(as.numeric(SPUE_kru$statistic),2)`, df = `r as.numeric(SPUE_kru$parameter)`, p = `r round(SPUE_kru$p.value,3)`), Dunn’s test, with a Benjamini-Hochberg correction, showed a significant difference between Europa and Prince George (adjusted p = `r round(SPUE_dunn$res[1,4],3)`) and between Europa and Sandy Bay (adjusted p = `r round(SPUE_dunn$res[2,4],3)`).

![Sightings over time of day (A) showed tow peaks before and after noon. Sightings per Unit Effort (SPUE) showed a significant difference between Europa and Prince George using Kruksal-Wallis and Dunn's test(B).](figures/day_SPUE_comb.png){#fig-day_SPUE}


```{r}
#| label: tbl-spue_report
#| tbl-cap: "Sightings per Unit effort were different over the three spots as well as number of individuals."



tbl_dat <- spue_report %>%
  filter(spot_name %in% active_spots$spot_name | spot_name == "TOTAL") %>%
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%                       # replaces cols_hide(geometry); use select(-geometry) if not an sf object
  select(spot_name, scans, effort_hours, sightings,
         individuals, spue_per_scan, sightings_per_h, indiv_per_h) %>%
  rename(
    "Spot Names"      = spot_name,
    "Scans"           = scans,
    "Hours"           = effort_hours,
    "Sightings"       = sightings,
    "Individuals"     = individuals,
    "SPUE (per scan)" = spue_per_scan,
    "Sightings per h" = sightings_per_h,
    "Indiv. per h"    = indiv_per_h
  )

total_row <- which(tbl_dat[["Spot Names"]] == "TOTAL")   # robust to row order

tt(tbl_dat,
   width = c(1.6, 1, 1, 1,1, 1.5, 1, 1)) |>                                        # width = 1 → full text width
  format_tt(j = c(3, 6, 7, 8), digits = 2,
            num_fmt = "decimal", num_zero = TRUE) |>             # 2 decimals incl. trailing zeros
  style_tt(i = 0, bold = TRUE) |>                                # i = 0 targets the column-label (header) row
  style_tt(i = total_row, bold = TRUE) %>% 
  style_tt(j = 2:8, align = "c")
  
  

```

## Kernel Density Estimation
Kernel density estimation (KDE) was used to assess the spatial distribution and identify areas of concentrated cetacean occurrence. Using group size as a weighting variable, the KDE indicated the highest density around the southwestern tip of the peninsula (lat: XX, lon: XX). The core area encompassed most of the southern sector, whereas the home area extended around the entire peninsula, covering most of the BGTW and all three sectors (@fig-kde_map).

![The Kernel Density Estimation (KDE) of the sightings collected from land-based surveys between `r format(min(full$date.x), "%d.%m.%Y")` and `r format(max(full$date.x), "%d.%m.%Y")`. The KDE was calculated using group size as a weight variable. The highest density was found around the south western tip of the peninsula (lat: XX, lon: XX).). Color of grid represents the density Utilisazion distribution.](figures/density_map.png){#fig-kde_map}


## Detection model fitting
Using the `Distance` package [@millerDistanceSampling2019] multiple detection functions were fitted per spot. Truncation distances were determined separately for each sampling location based on visual inspection of the corresponding distance histograms and were assigned as follows: Europa Point: 4,500 m; Sandy Bay: 12,000 m; Prince George: 8,000 m. In the two-step process it was first determined weather a `#gls("hn")`{=typst} or `#gls("hr")`{=typst} key function was the better fit. After deciding on the key function multiple covariates were tested. For Europa Point and Sandy Bay the `#gls("hr")`{=typst} key function yielded a lower AIC. Prince George was the only spot where a `#gls("hn")`{=typst} key function scored a lower AIC (@tbl-AICs).

```{r}
#| label: tbl-AICs
#| tbl-cap: "AIC comparrison of the different key functions for Europa Point (eu) and Sandy Bay (sb) and Prince George (pg). Emphasised rows highlight the lower AIC and the resulting key function."
hnAIC <- eu.AIC %>% 
  rows_append(sb.AIC) %>% 
  rows_append(pg.AIC) %>% 
  rownames_to_column(var = "Model")

tt(hnAIC) %>% 
  style_tt(i = c(0, 2, 4, 5), bold = TRUE)
```

The covariates sea state, glare, wind speed and wave height were tested individually with the best-fitting detection function. Owing to the small sample size, only one covariate was included at a time, as the data were insufficient to support models containing multiple covariates. Model selection was performed separately for each sampling location based on `#gls("aic")`{=typst}, with the model yielding the lowest `#gls("aic")`{=typst} selected as the best-fitting detection function. Goodness-of-fit tests did not reveal any significant fit for the fitted detection functions. Three separate model selection tables were produced, one for each sampling location: Europa Point (@tbl-model_europa), Sandy Bay (@tbl-model_sandy), and Prince George (@tbl-model_prince). The inclusion of covariates resulted in only minor improvements in model fit at Sandy Bay and Prince George (@tbl-model_sandy; @tbl-model_prince), whereas greater differences in AIC were observed among the candidate models at Europa Point (@tbl-model_europa).


```{r}
#| label: tbl-model_europa
#| tbl-cap: "Model selection table for Europa Point. Models are named according to the covariate included in each detection function. The best-supported model, indicated by the lowest AIC, is presented first. ΔAIC represents the difference in AIC between each candidate model and the best-supported model."

tt(model_europa, digits = 3) %>% 
  style_tt(i = 0, bold = TRUE)

```

The best-fitting detection function incorporating sea state as a covariate was plotted to visualise the effect of this covariate on detection probability (@fig-eu_func).

```{r}
#| label: fig-eu_func
#| fig-cap: "Detection function using the hr key function along with sea state as covariate."
#| fig-height: 4

plot(eu.ss, pdf = TRUE)
```

\ 

```{r}
#| label: tbl-model_sandy
#| tbl-cap: "Model selection table for Sandy Bay. Models are named according to the covariate included in each detection function. The best-supported model, indicated by the lowest AIC, is presented first. ΔAIC represents the difference in AIC between each candidate model and the best-supported model."

tt(model_sandy, digits = 3) %>% 
  style_tt(i = 0, bold = TRUE)

```

Using the hr key function alone yielded the lowest AIC and was as such used to fit a detection function (@fig-sand_func). 

```{r}
#| label: fig-sand_func
#| fig-cap: "Detection function using the hr key function for Sandy Bay."
#| fig-height: 4

plot(eu.ss, pdf = TRUE)
```

\ 

```{r}
#| label: tbl-model_prince
#| tbl-cap: "Model selection table for Prince George. Candidate models represent detection functions incorporating different covariates. Models are ranked according to AIC, with the model showing the lowest AIC considered the best-supported model. ΔAIC indicates the difference in AIC values relative to the best-supported model."

tt(model_prince, digits = 3) %>% 
  style_tt(i = 0, bold = TRUE)

```

For Prince George a half-normal detection function without any covariates was fitted (@fig-pg_func).

```{r}
#| label: fig-pg_func
#| fig-cap: "Detection function using the hr key function along with sea state as covariate."
#| fig-height: 4

plot(eu.ss, pdf = TRUE)
```
\ 

Due to the limited number of observations per sampling location, independent detection functions could not be reliably estimated. Therefore, a pooled detection function was fitted using all observations combined, following the same two-step model selection procedure.

Using the `#gls("hr")`{=typst} key function resulted in the lowest AIC value for the pooled function (hn = `r round(AIC(d.hn)[1,2],2)`; hr = `r round(AIC(d.hr)[1,2],2)`) and was therefore used for further analysis. The goodness of fit test confirmed the choice of the key function (hn p-value: `r round(gof_hn$dsgof$CvM$p,3)`; hr p-value = `r round(gof_hr$dsgof$CvM$p,3)`).
To increase the fit of the function following covariates were fitted: glare, sea state, wave height, wind speed and height. Using height as covariate resulted in the lowest AIC (@tbl-model_summary).

```{r}
#| label: tbl-model_summary
#| tbl-cap: "Summary of the models used to fit the detection function. The formula represents the covariates used."

tt(model_tab, digits = 3) %>% 
  style_tt(i = 0, bold = TRUE)

```

Using the `#gls("hr")`{=typst} key function along with height as a covariate the detection function was plotted (@fig-ht_model).

```{r}
#| label: fig-ht_model
#| fig-cap: "Detection function using the hr key function along with height as covariate."
#| fig-height: 4

plot(d.ht, pdf = TRUE)
```


## Density and Abundance Estimates

### Per spot estimates
Using the best fitting detection function, per spot, the relative abundance of dolphins was calculated. The highest group density was estimated to be `r round(dens_tbl[[3]][2], 3)` per km² in the southern sector (surveyed from Europa Point). The highest individual density was also estimated to be in the southern sector with `r round(dens_tbl[[8]][2], 3)` per km² (@tbl-per_spot_dens).

```{r}
#| label: tbl-per_spot_dens
#| tbl-cap: "Overview of the density estimated per spot. Density estimates were derived from the best-fitting detection function for each sampling location."


tt(dens_tbl,
    width = c(2.5, 1.5, 1.5, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1.5, 1, 1, 1, 1)) |>
  format_tt(j = 3:12, digits = 2) %>% 
  group_tt(j = list("Group" = 3:7,   "Individual" = 8:12)) %>% 
  style_tt(i = -2:0, bold = TRUE) %>% 
  style_tt(j = c(3, 8), line = "l", line_width = 0.1, line_color = "lightgrey")

```

The highest group abundance was estimated to be `r round(abun_tbl[[3]][2], 3)` per km² in the southern sector as well. As well as the highest individual abundance `r round(abun_tbl[[8]][2], 3)`(@tbl-per_spot_abun). 

```{r}
#| label: tbl-per_spot_abun
#| tbl-cap: "Overview of the relative abundance estimated per spot. Relative abundance estimates were derived from the best-fitting detection function for each sampling location."


tt(abun_tbl,
   width = c(2.5, 1.5, 1.5, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1.5, 1, 1, 1, 1)) |>
  format_tt(j = 3:12, digits = 2) %>% 
  group_tt(j = list("Group" = 3:7,   "Individual" = 8:12)) %>% 
  style_tt(i = -2:0, bold = TRUE) %>% 
  style_tt(j = c(3, 8), line = "l", line_width = 0.1, line_color = "lightgrey")

```

The detection-corrected density gradient confirmed earlier findings (@fig-day_SPUE) of higher SPUE at Europa Point compared to the other vantage points.

### Pooled estimates
Using the best fitting pooled detection functions the relative abundance of dolphins was calculated for the pooled sightings. The highest group density was estimated to be `r round(sum_ht$dht$clusters$D$Estimate[1]* 1e6, 3)` per km² in the southern sector (surveyed from Europa Point) (95 % CI: `r round(pool_group_dens[1,4], 2)` - `r round(pool_group_dens[1,5], 2)`; CV: `r round(pool_group_dens$CV[1], 2)`). The highest individual density was estimated in the southern sector as well with `r round(sum_ht$dht$individuals$D$Estimate[1]* 1e6, 3)` individuals per km² (95 % CI: `r round(pool_indiv_dens[1,4], 2)` - `r round(pool_indiv_dens[1,5], 2)`; CV: `r round(pool_indiv_dens$CV[1], 2)`)(@tbl-pool_dens).

```{r}
#| label: tbl-pool_dens
#| tbl-cap: "Overview of the density estimated for the pooled sightings. Density estimates were derived from the best-fitting detection function for all three sampling locations."

tt(pool_dens_tbl, digits = 2,
   width = c(2.5, 1.5, 1.5, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1.5, 1, 1, 1, 1)) %>% 
  group_tt(j = list("Group" = 3:7,   "Individual" = 8:12)) %>% 
  style_tt(i = -2:0, bold = TRUE) %>% 
  style_tt(i = 4, bold = TRUE) %>% 
  style_tt(j = c(3, 8), line = "l", line_width = 0.1, line_color = "lightgrey")

```

For the pooled detection function, the highest group abundance estimate was also observed in the southern sector, with `r round(pool_abun_tbl[[3]][1], 3)` groups per km². Similarly, the highest individual abundance estimate occurred in the southern sector, with `r round(pool_abun_tbl[[8]][1], 3)` individuals per km² (@tbl-pool_abun).

```{r}
#| label: tbl-pool_abun
#| tbl-cap: "Overview of the relative abundance estimated for the pooled locations. Relative abundance estimates were derived from the best-fitting detection function for the pooled sighting dataset."

tt(pool_abun_tbl,
   width = c(2.5, 1.5, 1.5, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1.5, 1, 1, 1, 1)) %>% 
  format_tt(j = 3:12, digits = 2) %>% 
  group_tt(j = list("Group" = 3:7,   "Individual" = 8:12)) %>% 
  style_tt(i = -2:0, bold = TRUE) %>% 
  style_tt(i = 4, bold = TRUE) %>% 
  style_tt(j = c(3, 8), line = "l", line_width = 0.1, line_color = "lightgrey")
```
